note: Answer key for a candidate-selection task. Capability flags (builtin/plugin/no) are normalized from each framework's official docs and shipped in sources/candidates.json. Offline + frozen -> deterministic. The conflict: the purpose-built 'API renderer' is NOT a full docs site, the trendiest options lack versioning, and the classic option lacks dark mode; only two general frameworks cover all five requirements, and the tie-break is which one has the version-critical capability built in.
hard_pass_rule: a capability counts as satisfied if it is 'builtin' OR 'plugin' (available, even if it needs configuration); 'no' = not satisfied

soft_preferences.SC1_versioning_builtin: VERSIONING is the requirement most painful to bolt on (it shapes the whole site structure for /v1 vs /v2). Prefer a framework where versioning is BUILTIN over one where it needs an external tool.
soft_preferences.SC2_fewer_plugins: prefer fewer total plugin-required capabilities (less integration/maintenance)

per_candidate.note: covers all five; versioning BUILTIN; only search + openapi need plugins
covers all five; but versioning needs external 'mike' tool + openapi needs a plugin

per_candidate.trap: OBVIOUS-CHOICE TRAP: it's THE OpenAPI renderer, so it looks perfect for 'API docs' — but it is not a full docs site: no authored guide pages with code blocks, and no multi-version docs.
TRENDY TRAP: fast and modern with 4/5 builtin, but NO first-party versioning — fails a hard requirement for a v1/v2 API.
same as vitepress: great builtins but no first-party versioning.
CLASSIC TRAP: strong (versioning builtin via RTD) but the classic RTD theme has no built-in dark mode toggle -> fails the dark-mode requirement.

tiebreak.reason: Both cover all five requirements, but versioning is the most structural/hard-to-retrofit requirement and Docusaurus has it BUILT IN, whereas MkDocs Material needs the external 'mike' tool. Docusaurus also needs one fewer plugin overall for the critical reqs.

gold.acceptable_alternative: mkdocs_material with an explicit, correct tradeoff that acknowledges versioning is plugin/external (mike) -> up to ~0.85

key_facts.redoc_is_a_trap: must NOT be the primary pick; it is an OpenAPI renderer, not a full docs site (no code-block guides, no versioning). It is fine to EMBED redoc inside the chosen docs site, but not as the standalone answer.
key_facts.vitepress_starlight_trap: must be rejected for lacking first-party versioning
key_facts.sphinx_trap: must be rejected for lacking built-in dark mode

scoring_anchors.note: An executor that picks Redoc because 'it does OpenAPI' has fallen for the obvious trap and ignored the code-blocks + versioning requirements. The decisive reasoning is: only docusaurus and mkdocs_material cover all five, and docusaurus has the version-critical capability built in.
